close_reason: Compiler2 now lowers case/with/cond through dispatch_matrix and verifies the surface through fz2 plus materialization regressions.
description:
Goal: lower source control constructs that choose among user arms into Compiler2-owned structured control facts driven by `dispatch_matrix`.

Signal:
- representative fixtures using `case` and `with` run through `fz2`
- no Compiler2 path reopens old `ir_lower` matcher helpers or planner-side dispatch machinery

Change:
- compile clause rows into `dispatch_matrix` plans inside Compiler2
- lower per-arm bodies and failure paths (`case_clause`, `with_clause`) against the explicit structured control model from `fz-rh2.11.1`
- cover `cond` in the same design, even if current fixture surface for it is thin

Why:
- `case` and `with` are ordinary language surface in the fixture corpus, and `dispatch_matrix` already owns the hard part: arm selection and bindings
title: Compiler2 control lowering: lower case/with/cond through dispatch_matrix
